item.title: JMS remains a delight
item.description:
And if you don't know the people in question:
If you're old enough to have watched 80s cartoons or shows growing up, or were a comics reader in the 90s or 2000s, there's a pretty good chance you've enjoyed the works of JMS, as he did a lot of writing on both cartoon and live action tv during the 80s and was everywhere in the comics industry during the late 90s-2000s, as he seemed to be writing every other major comic series of the time.
And then there's this POS.
item.dc:creator: PoliticalWanderer

item.title: the harpy's meme library: volume 54 (applauding molecules)
item.description:
Hello and welcome to another meme dump, my imgur friends. The above meme is absolutely correct, I've always tweaked every recipe I've ever made to have more cheese than called for, because they NEVER use enough cheese and cheese is fucking amazing
Otto and me, anxiety thundershirt buddies forever
I have had strangers stab me with needles for hours on end and yet I still absolutely hate answering phones
oh nooo how terrible
Unless it's a typical suburban lawn/golf course. THAT grass is an abomination.
Just remember your brain is an organ just like every other one in your body. Sometimes it can be wonky and need a little help, and that's okay
The internet people like when I do this, does that count as being a productive member of society?
That's me today, as a whole ass adult
Yeah I always wanted to be cool and mysterious. Turns out I'm just a weird goth-flavored clown
I'm already going to hell so I don't feel bad laughing at Titan memes, they get me every single time All right, that's all for this meme dump, my imgur friends. Until next time, don't forget to be awesome!
item.dc:creator: SecretHarpy

item.title: Good on yah
item.description: Some people say it's nothing for them. Others wish it didn't have to be this way. At some point some of us question if we made a mistake. Maybe they've changed. No shame in asking that. No contact doesn't have to be permanent. Don't feel ashamed for having empathy for others. If they've changed and it took your absence I'm happy for you. If they haven't and you need to go back to no contact, you did nothing wrong by trying to reconnect. You weren't the reason they hurt you. Stop relying on the resiliency of children for your shitty parenting. Because that resilience will break someday. We aren't an insurance policy or a retirement plan. We're people who are forced to live through your trauma. Pull yourself up by your bootstraps and go get therapy. It's not anyone else's responsibility to deal with your shit. Isn't that what you tell us all the time? Anyway, for the kids struggling with no contact, you're brilliant, you're valid, you didn't deserve the neglect and abuse. From a mom who is breaking the cycle, I'm so proud of you. Edit: for a bit of backstory for myself my mom would have assaulted me if it weren't for my brother protecting me. This happened at a public venue. Security got involved and offered to have her arrested. She was so drunk she doesn't remember anything. I empathize with the parents who have toxic children. With the parents who don't understand and would actually do whatever it is their children would need. I understand people can weaponize NC. As someone who needed to do it for my safety I am addressing anyone who has had to do the same as me. I can only imagine how painful it is to hear people congratulating others for doing something that has hurt you. I'm sorry. That is not my intention. I hope you're able to navigate this pain and heal. Maybe on your journey you and the ones you lost can reconnect. Like I said it doesn't have to be permanent. My mom recently told a family member she believes she's dead to me. She's not. There have been moments I've considered trying to reengage. Her saying this only demonstrates she won't change. She won't even hear me out. The person who tried talking to her about their issues realized that and told me. It sucks. I won't say NC ruined any chance of reconciliation. There never was a chance. She may feel punished, but ignores this is a result of her actions. But that's my experience. And by no means universal. I honestly wish no one ever had to choose NC. That people could own up and change. That's not reality. Perhaps this post is your invitation to reflect on what has happened. What have your children, or anyone really, been saying that you may have ignored? NC doesn't just happen. There is a portion of people who use it to manipulate or avoid addressing issues. Maybe that's your situation. Idk. I don't know you. Only you know you. And you don't need to prove anything to me or anyone else here. This is a rhetorical question. May everyone find healing.
item.dc:creator: Wrongclusterbdisorderbutgooff

item.dc:creator: DropDrop

item.title: Climate dump! Come in for your gloom and doom
item.description:
Self-explanatory. It's already out of date, but it shows the massive spike in temperature that started happening ~150 years ago (most of which in the last 40 years, that little bump is WW2.)
AMOC collapse is "pretty likely by 2030-2050". It would cause all of Europe to basically collapse. Temperatures would 'concentrate' in a band across central Europe. France would be "okay", but hundreds of millions would likely die. No worries.
"EEI", Earth Energy Imbalance, is a fairly new concept for commoners like me. But it shows the reason for the massive bump in "energy the earth got from the sun" we got the past ~1.5 years. Something "broke" in how the globe absorbed radiation from the sun in 2023. Likely "IMO2020", cleaner shipping fuel that were causing massive amounts of clouds (cooling effect) over the world's oceans. We had been hiding a BUNCH of warming up until then, and... here it is.
This is how much fossil fuels we extract from faaaar under the ground each year now. Oh, and it's still increasing. I actually have a sort of "metaphor" for this pic. If the atmosphere had the same pressure as at sea level (us) all the way up, it would only be 8km tall (5 miles). Normally the air gets thinner the higher you go, but not in this metaphor. In this image you see Burj Khalifa, which is 800m tall (0.5 miles). So the atmosphere is practically just 10 times higher than that building! As we pump up CO2 (and other planet heating gases), it just... fills up. It doesn't "go" anywhere. It just spreads out across this incredibly thin layer of gas we call the atmosphere on this planet.
Right, this is the IMO2020 stuff I talked about, so brand new science! The red represents the places we *WERE* creating clouds using the dirty shipping fuel, that was used in all the world's cargo ships. Oh, and that dirty fuel is sulfur. Incredibly bad for human health (which is why we regulated it). Those clouds are now mostly gone, and because of that, ocean temperatures have spiked, revealing the real global warming we had caused. Oh, and hurricane Beryl was likely a consequence of this.
More than 1000 dead in the pilgrimage to Mecka earlier this year, due to record heat.
Some basics (skip if you know how CO2 works). On the surface of the planet, there's always been a relatively stable amount of carbon "available". The carbon moves around in the "carbon cycle", so from the air, to the oceans, to all the vegetation on land, in a cycle that keeps repeating. But once you start messing with fossil fuels, ancient *BURIED* sources of oil, coal and gas, we eventually (like 200 years ago) realized that those carbon sources hadn't been part of the surface of the planet for likely hundreds of millions of years. And suddenly we're "shocking" the planet with extra carbon that just wasn't there before. Remember those huge cubes? We burn those each year, and turn them into giant clouds of CO2 (carbon dioxide), using the atmosphere as a dump for our pollution. And since CO2 just builds up in the atmosphere, it obviously has effects on global warming, and the climate (the sum of all weather). The world will keep heating up as long as we use fossil fuels for our machines. Cars, trucks, planes, coal and gas plants, the works. Nobody thinks it'll be an easy change to make, but the alternative, NOT changing, will absolutely lead us to doom as crops start failing on a global scale, unable to feed 8.1 billion people.
We're hurting the animal kingdom with our way of life.
Ah, in Swedish, but I'll explain. It shows the emissions of various countries. "India, "All other countries", USA, China. But the rightmost one shows "All countries with less than 2% of the total emissions", adding up to a whopping 40% of all emissions. It's showing that small countries definitely have their fair share of the blame here. In the end it's behavior that matters, not country size. If you have a shopping mall and cars near you, your country *is* part of the problem.
Just a heatwave on the south pole causing apocalyptic heat there, nothing to worry about I'm sure............ Unless you care about the "doomsday glacier", Thwaites, about to slide into the ocean and raise sea levels by several feet, wiping out many, many cities. But hey, that's at least 20 (!!) years into the future! "Yawn" am I right?
Oh right, the north pole! So, the north pole, the Arctic, is just ice that's floating on the ocean there. There's no *land* under the ice like on the south pole (Antarctic). Funny story. Antarctic means "no polar bears here". So if you ever mix them up, just think "anti polar bears". Anyway, remember how I said clouds cooled the planet, and that we got rid of a bunch of clouds over the oceans recently, causing a spike.....? Ringing any bells? Well, anything "white" on the planet actually cools us, a lot. So it's bad news when "white stuff" disappears. Yeah, the north pole is about to completely melt, as it's just a thin layer of ever melting ice right now. Most of it is less than 1 meter thick (4 feet), and it could go within 10 years, causing *yet another spike* in global temperature. And I'm thinking it'll be a BIG one.
Yesterday, this scientist figured out that we've practically doubled the amount of greenhouse gases in the atmosphere, if you account for other powerful gases we're also responsible for (methane and nitrous oxide). If you just count CO2, it's "just" 50% more. It's bad. Whatever hope you have, at least realize that we've made our bed, and it's very soon time to lay in it. It's so bad.
item.dc:creator: Ree81

item.title: Time for yet another unhinged XL Sunday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
greetings online friends
looks too fancy for me
you KNOW he's thinking about some pizza
crunchy
i'm on team derek
flowers are a little easier to acquire
that was nice of them to leave a thank you note
that looks like too much sugar to me and i love sugar
mmm hmm
that would be cool
peent
classic steve
but 3019 hasn't happened yet.. and they wouldn't need to colorize it
as is tradition
it's true, we are like that
so many stems and seeds
yay
that's my set.. have a great night gang!!

item.title: Um…*gestures vaguely*
item.description: It’s a contract job that has incremental downsizing. I work in quality so I have a job as long as we have a contract (no one else knows how to do my job). But the workload only takes about 20 minutes a day now and 2 more people are leaving. It’s been fun but I have to look out for myself.
